status.description: confirmed = the finding still reproduces. fixed = the original reproduction no longer triggers (upstream fix applied between original scan and this run). inconclusive = could not run the reproduction; explain why in notes. deferred = the reproduction was not run because preflight classified it EXTERNAL-REACH; a human must run it in an environment with a callback listener.

classification.description: local-safe = trigger uses only stdin/file input or connects only to loopback / a server the reproduction itself starts on loopback, and writes only under the workspace or OS temp. external-reach = resolves or connects to any other host, reads a credential file or credential env var, or writes outside the workspace and temp.

justification.description: The exact lines from the reproduction that decided the classification, quoted verbatim.

reproducer.description: The exact reproduction you ran, verbatim: the full script, command line, or input you executed against ./src — not a description of it. This is what makes the evidence interpretable; without it the output below is meaningless. Include the language/runner so the analyst can re-run it (e.g. the .rb/.py file contents, or the shell commands).

evidence.description: The output produced by running the reproducer above: stdout, stderr, exit code, failing assertion, diff of behaviour. Paste the relevant excerpts, not a summary.

notes.description: Short prose explaining what changed since the original scan, or why the reproduction was inconclusive or deferred. When status is inconclusive because a runtime is missing, prefix with 'env-blocked:' so the operator knows to fix the container profile rather than re-read the finding.
